diagnostics.message: useEffect subscribes via `addEventListener(...)` but never returns a cleanup — leaks the registration on every re-run and on unmount. Return a cleanup function that calls the matching remove/unsubscribe call
diagnostics.help: Return a cleanup function that releases the subscription / timer: `return () => target.removeEventListener(name, handler)` for listeners, `return () => clearInterval(id)` / `clearTimeout(id)` for timers, or `return unsubscribe` if the subscribe call already returned one

diagnostics.message: "touchstart" listener without { passive: true } — blocks scrolling performance. Only add { passive: true } if the handler does NOT call event.preventDefault() (passive listeners silently ignore preventDefault())
diagnostics.help: Add `{ passive: true }` as the third argument: `addEventListener('scroll', handler, { passive: true })`. Only do this if the handler does NOT call `event.preventDefault()` — passive listeners silently ignore `preventDefault()`, which breaks features like pull-to-refresh suppression, custom gestures, and nested-scroll containment.

diagnostics.message: useEffect subscribes via `addEventListener(...)` but never returns a cleanup — leaks the registration on every re-run and on unmount. Return a cleanup function that calls the matching remove/unsubscribe call
diagnostics.help: Return a cleanup function that releases the subscription / timer: `return () => target.removeEventListener(name, handler)` for listeners, `return () => clearInterval(id)` / `clearTimeout(id)` for timers, or `return unsubscribe` if the subscribe call already returned one
